message:
docs: Fix cache terminology (System-Level Cache, not L2)

Corrected the technical explanation of Apple Silicon's cache hierarchy.
The shared cache between CPU, GPU, and NPU is the System-Level Cache
(SLC, 8-32MB), not the L2 cache.

Also added Q-Healing to key innovations to emphasize the quality+speed story.

files.patch:
@@ -202,35 +202,49 @@ This enables running **Qwen2.5-Coder-32B** (32B params) in just **12GB RAM** on
 - KV Cache: 2GB (1024MB per cache)
 - **Total: 12GB** (fits in 16GB Mac with room to spare!)
 
-**The Secret Sauce: Shared L2 Cache**
+**The Secret Sauce: Unified Memory + System-Level Cache**
 
 This is what makes Zell's 3-way heterogeneous architecture possible:
 
-1. **GPU dequantizes** Q2_K→BF16 and writes to L2 cache (16MB shared)
-2. **NPU matmul** reads BF16 data directly from L2 (cache hit!)
-3. **No DRAM access** - 155GB/s effective bandwidth = L2 speed
-4. **All three units** (CPU, GPU, NPU) share the same L2 cache
+1. **GPU dequantizes** Q2_K→BF16 and writes to System-Level Cache (8-32MB shared)
+2. **NPU matmul** reads BF16 data directly from SLC (cache hit!)
+3. **Minimal DRAM access** - Data flows through shared cache hierarchy
+4. **All three units** (CPU, GPU, NPU) share unified memory AND system cache
+
+**Apple Silicon's Unique Architecture:**
+
+```
+CPU: L1 (128KB) → L2 (16MB, CPU-only) ──┐
+                                         ├──> System-Level Cache (8-32MB) ──> Unified RAM (32GB)
+GPU: L1 (8KB)   → L2 (3MB, GPU-only)  ──┤
+                                         │
+NPU: (Neural Engine, 16 TOPS) ──────────┘
+```
 
 **Why This Only Works on Apple Silicon:**
 
-| Platform | CPU Cache | GPU Cache | NPU Cache | Shared? |
-|----------|-----------|-----------|-----------|---------|
-| **Apple Silicon** | **L2 (16MB)** | **L2 (16MB)** | **L2 (16MB)** | **✅ YES!** |
-| NVIDIA GPU | L2 | Separate L2 | N/A | ❌ No |
-| AMD GPU | L2 | Separate L2 | N/A | ❌ No |
-| Intel Arc GPU | L2 | Shared memory | N/A | ⚠️ Memory only, not cache |
+| Platform | Unified Memory | System-Level Cache | NPU | Can Replicate? |
+|----------|----------------|-------------------|-----|----------------|
+| **Apple Silicon** | **✅ Yes** | **✅ Shared (8-32MB)** | **✅ 16 TOPS** | **N/A (Original)** |
+| NVIDIA GPU | ❌ Separate CPU/GPU | ❌ No SLC | ❌ No NPU | ❌ **Impossible** |
+| AMD GPU | ❌ Separate CPU/GPU | ❌ No SLC | ❌ No NPU | ❌ **Impossible** |
+| Intel Arc | ⚠️ Shared memory | ❌ Separate caches | ❌ No NPU | ❌ **Impossible** |
+| Qualcomm | ⚠️ Unified memory | ⚠️ Different hierarchy | ✅ Has NPU | ⚠️ **Maybe (unproven)** |
 
-Traditional GPUs require explicit memory copies between CPU and GPU. Intel has unified *memory* but not unified *cache*. **Only Apple Silicon shares L2 cache between all three compute units.**
+Traditional GPUs require explicit memory copies between CPU and GPU memory pools. Intel has unified memory but separate cache hierarchies. **Only Apple Silicon combines unified memory + shared system cache + Neural Engine.**
 
 **Key Innovations:**
 - **Q2_K compression** - 2.96 bpw: 32B model from 64GB → 10GB
+- **Q-Healing LoRA** - Fixes quantization errors, Q2_K quality → Q8_0 quality
 - **All layers resident** - No SSD streaming, zero page faults
 - **GPU dequantization** - Metal kernels (40x faster than CPU SIMD)
 - **MPSGraph for matmul** - Automatic Neural Engine routing via [Metal Performance Shaders Graph](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/metalperformanceshadersgraph)
-- **Shared L2 cache** - THE competitive advantage (Apple Silicon only)
+- **Shared system cache** - THE competitive advantage (Apple Silicon only)
 - **Unified memory** - Zero-copy buffers between all compute units
 - **3-way parallelism** - CPU, GPU, NPU all working simultaneously
 
+**Result: Better quality (Q8_0 level) + 38x faster + same memory (12GB)**
+
 **The Competitive Moat:**
 
 This architecture is **impossible to replicate** on other platforms:
